latest.title: Financial Music Limited - Form 8.3 - AFERIAN plc
latest.content:
Today 17:42
RNS Number : 0101V
Financial Music Limited
02 March 2026
FORM 8.3
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY
A PERSON WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE
Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Full name of discloser:
FINANCIAL MUSIC LIMITED
(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):
The naming of nominee or vehicle companies is insufficient. For a trust, the trustee(s), settlor and beneficiaries must be named.
VICTORIA STREATER AND CLOSE RELATIVES
(c) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
AFERIAN PLC
(d) If an exempt fund manager connected with an offeror/offeree, state this and specify identity of offeror/offeree:
(e) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
27/02/2026
(f) In addition to the company in 1(c) above, is the discloser making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state
"N/A"
N/A
2. POSITIONS OF THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
1p Ordinary Shares
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
995022
0.89
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
995022
0.89
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchase/sale
Number of securities
Price per unit
1p ORDINARY SHARES
SALE
500000
0.008865p
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
NO
Date of disclosure:
02/03/2026
Contact name:
VICTORIA STREATER
Telephone number*:
07775 710 852
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.
The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
*If the discloser is a natural person, a telephone number does not need to be included, provided contact information has been provided to the Panel's Market Surveillance Unit.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.

company_30s.what_they_do: AFRN.L runs a niche music‑licensing and distribution platform that helps independent artists place their tracks in advertising, film, and gaming projects. It also offers royalty‑collection services for creators who need a reliable way to track and collect income from digital streams.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The music‑licensing space is growing as brands look for fresh, affordable audio content. AFRN’s technology gives small artists a foothold in a market that used to be dominated by large labels, and its data‑driven approach appeals to advertisers who want to measure impact. The company sits in a sector that is still expanding, so a successful pivot could unlock significant value.
company_30s.current_state: AFRN is still in a cash‑burn phase with limited revenue streams. The company is trying to secure additional financing and is actively courting brand partners. It trades at a very low price relative to its valuation, reflecting the uncertainty around its future cash flow.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show AFRN is extending its senior banking facilities and providing a brief trading update, indicating it is still able to service debt and keep operations running.
A Form 8.3 filing about a related entity, Financial Music Limited, suggests the company is exploring new revenue channels through music‑investment vehicles.
Together, these documents paint a picture of a company that is keeping its doors open while looking for fresh capital and partnership opportunities.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: A quiet, watch‑and‑wait vibe – traders are not yet excited but are keeping an eye on any sign of accumulation.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the shrinking free float and recent comments about ‘delayed buy’ activity as signs that institutional buyers are quietly piling in.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the tiny float and lack of clear revenue make the stock a high‑risk play that could fall to zero if financing falls through.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from neutral to mildly bullish over the last few hours, but the overall conversation remains muted.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no public interest in the ticker, meaning the market is largely unaware and any positive news could trigger a surprise rally.

near_term_catalysts: A successful closing of the extended banking facility or a new equity round would provide the liquidity needed to support operations.
A partnership announcement with a major streaming or advertising platform would give the company a credible revenue source.
Positive cash‑flow figures in a future trading update could signal the start of a turnaround.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to look for are a 150 % jump in daily volume, a break above the current 52‑week low, and any statement from management about a new revenue stream or funding.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early post‑capitulation phase. It is still waiting for a clear catalyst that will convince the market it can move out of the deep‑crash zone.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If AFRN manages to secure fresh funding or a new partnership, its price could climb to the midpoint of its historic range, delivering a 200 %+ upside from the current level. The accumulation chatter suggests smart money is already buying in, which could accelerate the rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company cannot raise enough capital and continues to burn cash, the price could fall to zero or trigger a delisting. The lack of a clear revenue engine makes this a real threat.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price; potential gain could be three to five times that amount, giving a clear 3:1 reward‑to‑risk profile.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a 20 % dip from the current level, size the position at 5–10 % of the portfolio, and place a stop 10 % below the entry to protect against a sudden drop.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume spike of at least 150 % and a move above the 50‑day simple moving average – that would be a clear sign the market is turning.
playbook.if_passing: None identified; the main risks are liquidity and funding, not immediate bankruptcy or fraud.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AFRN.L sits in a deep crash with no public buzz – a classic contrarian setup that could pay handsomely if a financing or partnership catalyst arrives, but risks a continued decline if liquidity dries out.
